814.623.4807 - Fax - 814.623.0991 Ayme Eichenlaub, Director of Elections aeichenlaub@bedfordcountypa.org Olivia Brenize, Assistant to the Director of Elections obrenize@bedfordcountypa.org Bedford County Board of Elections Deb Baughman, Chairman Mike Stiles J R Winck General Election 2026 DEADLINES DEADLINE FOR REGISTERING TO VOTE is October 19, 2026 DEADLINE TO APPLY FOR AN ABSENTEE/MAIL-IN BALLOT is October 27 2026 BALLOTS ARE DUE BACK by November 3, 2026, by ELECTION DAY IS TUESDAY, November 3, 2026 POLLING PLACES ARE OPEN FROM to NEWS & UPDATES In accordance with Pennsylvania Election Code 25 P.S. § 3031.10 (d), the Bedford County Board of Elections will be starting the preparation of our voting system and its components. The preparation includes the logic and accuracy testing of the Dominion precinct scanners.  The testing will be scheduled at a later date and may last all day, and will take place at the Bedford County Courthouse, Room 101. It is hard to determine how long it will take until all machines to be processed. Notice of Pre-Canvassing and Canvassing - The Bedford County Board of Elections will meet at approximately 8:00 a.m. on Tuesday November 3, 2026 in the Bedford County Courthouse Conference Room 101 located at 200 S. Juliana Street, Bedford, PA to pre-canvass mail-in and absentee ballots cast for the Municipal Election held on Tuesday November 3, 2026. - By order of the Bedford County Court of Common Pleas, the Bedford County Board of Elections will meet at approximately 9:00 a.m. on Friday November 6, 2026, in the Commissioners Suite and Room 303 located at 200 S. Juliana St., Bedford, PA to Canvass the votes cast at the Municipal Election. In response to numerous calls received by our office, we felt that it would be beneficial to provide the following information: Election Day is Tuesday, November 3, 2026 and polling locations will be open that day from - There is a misconception that Pennsylvania has Early Voting.  Early Voting is when a ballot is cast and counted prior to Election Day. Pennsylvania does not have Early Voting as our absentee/mail-in ballots are counted on Election Day. The public is welcome and encouraged to apply for their absentee/mail-in ballots now. We process these applications daily. If you will be leaving town and are unsure whether we will have ballots prior to when you leave, please call us and we will update you on the best course of action. Recently, several voter registration groups and political organizations have been sending blank absentee/mail-in applications.  If you would like to vote by absentee or mail-in ballot, only one application needs to be completed per election.  Interested voters can check their registration by going to the Department of State website - www.vote.pa.gov The Pennsylvania Attorney General's Office has provided important information regarding Voting Scams - Beware of Voting Scams - Pennsylvania Attorney General's Office The Bedford County Elections Office is responsible for ensuring the ability of eligible citizens of the County to be able to register and vote in fair and equal elections, as governed by law.  The Office of Elections and Voter Services cannot provide legal, tax, or accounting advice.  All candidates for public office should solicit advice from a licensed professional in the appropriate field in advance of statutory deadlines and before filing documents with this office. Election Ballot Drop Box Location Ballot Box The Elections Ballot Drop Box is located on the Penn Street side of the Courthouse Complex between the main building and the parking garage. The Drop Box will be available after mail-in, and absentee ballots have been mailed. The ballots in the Drop Box are retrieved multiple times during the day by two people.
